title: Reproduce the Vibrio nigripulchritudo multi-record circular genome in the web app
summary: Build a circular multi-record canvas for two chromosomes and four plasmids from a single RefSeq GBFF file, using automatic record sizing, explicit row placement, GC content, GC skew, and a left legend.
estimatedTime: 5-15 min after the input is available
requirements: The multi-record Vibrio nigripulchritudo GBFF file listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: GCF_015097735.1
downloads.note: Download the RefSeq genomic GBFF. The Gallery session uses six records from this file: NZ_AP024087.1 through NZ_AP024092.1.

quickReproduce.title: Download the Gallery session
quickReproduce.body: Use the Session button in this Gallery entry to save Vnig_TUMSAT-TG-2018.gbdraw-session.json.

media.alt: Gallery header for the Vibrio nigripulchritudo multi-record circular genome with the Session action visible.
media.caption: The Gallery entry keeps the finished interactive SVG, downloadable SVG, and session JSON together.

quickReproduce.title: Open the web app
quickReproduce.body: Open gbdraw.app, then load the saved .gbdraw-session.json from the session controls.

quickReproduce.title: Inspect the restored multi-record layout
quickReproduce.body: The session restores the GBFF input, circular mode, six-record canvas, automatic size scaling, row placement, title, legend, and generated interactive SVG.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Circular mode before adding the RefSeq GBFF input.

manualSteps.title: Upload the multi-record GBFF
manualSteps.body: Upload GCF_015097735.1_ASM1509773v1_genomic.gbff. The file contains two chromosomes and four plasmids.

manualSteps.title: Set the basic circular layout
manualSteps.body: Use the compact circular settings from the Gallery session.
items: Features: CDS, rRNA, tRNA, tmRNA, ncRNA, repeat_region

manualSteps.title: Enable Multi-record canvas
manualSteps.body: Turn on Multi-record canvas so all six records are drawn into one figure instead of separate output files.
manualSteps.note: The two large chromosomes sit in the top row. The four smaller plasmids share the second row.

manualSteps.title: Add the bottom title
manualSteps.body: Set the plot title to Vibrio nigripulchritudo TUMSAT-TG-2018, complete genome and place it at the bottom.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The preview should show chromosome 1 and chromosome 2 above plasmids pVNTG1 through pVNTG4, with GC content and GC skew rings inside each record.

media.alt: Generated Vibrio nigripulchritudo multi-record circular diagram in the Gallery preview.
media.caption: The finished figure uses automatic radius scaling so chromosomes and plasmids remain readable in one canvas.

manualSteps.title: Check the Files tab
manualSteps.body: Use the Gallery Files tab to confirm the required GBFF input, assembly source, session JSON, interactive SVG, and thumbnail.

media.alt: Vibrio nigripulchritudo Gallery Files tab listing the GBFF input download and Gallery artifacts.
media.caption: The Files tab shows that this multi-record figure starts from one GBFF input rather than six separate uploads.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a CDS feature
manualSteps.body: Click a CDS feature in the interactive SVG to inspect its label, record ID, location, protein_id, qualifiers, and sequence.

media.alt: Vibrio nigripulchritudo multi-record circular diagram with a CDS feature popup open.
media.caption: Feature clicks are useful for checking which chromosome or plasmid owns a selected annotation.

colorRules.title: This example uses palette defaults
colorRules.body: The main tutorial point is the multi-record layout. Feature colors use the orchid palette defaults.

colorRules.title: Use record layout before adding color complexity
colorRules.body: Confirm the six-record canvas, automatic sizing, and row placement before adding feature-specific colors or label changes.

colorRules.title: Choose qualifiers from feature clicks
colorRules.body: If you add feature color rules, click representative CDS features first and inspect gene, locus_tag, product, protein_id, and note values.

colorRules.title: Keep plasmid categories specific
colorRules.body: Use narrow product or locus_tag patterns when highlighting plasmid genes so chromosome annotations are not colored unintentionally.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click features to verify record ownership
postGenerationEdits.body: Feature popups show record ID and location, which is important when the same canvas contains chromosomes and plasmids.

postGenerationEdits.title: Keep the left legend compact
postGenerationEdits.body: The multi-record canvas already uses most of the width. If you edit the legend, keep it narrow enough that it does not cover chromosome 1.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export SVG first
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. Use PNG or PDF for quick sharing or draft figures.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore the GBFF input, multi-record placement, generated output, and SVG adjustments.

troubleshooting.title: Only one record is shown
troubleshooting.body: Confirm Multi-record canvas is enabled. Without that option, circular mode can render records separately instead of placing them in one canvas.

troubleshooting.title: The plasmids are too small
troubleshooting.body: Raise the minimum radius ratio, or switch size mode if you need less proportional scaling between chromosomes and plasmids.

troubleshooting.title: The rows differ from the Gallery figure
troubleshooting.body: Confirm record positions #1@1, #2@1, #3@2, #4@2, #5@2, and #6@2, plus column gap ratio 0.1 and row gap ratio 0.05.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Confirm the GBFF input, tuckin track type, orchid palette, separate strands, left legend, bottom title, multi-record size mode auto, and minimum radius ratio 0.55.
